refined_question: How can graph neural networks effectively leverage graph structure to improve node classification performance in scenarios with limited labeled nodes, without relying on additional labeling?
intent: Understand methods to mitigate overfitting in graph neural networks for node classification tasks with scarce labels, within the domain of graph representation learning and semi-supervised learning (SSL), focusing on exploiting graph structure
excluded_topics: Using large pre-trained models as a primary solution
Fine-tuning or transfer learning from large pre-trained models
Scaling parameter count as the main approach
search_hint: graph neural networks, semi-supervised learning, SSL, graph representation learning, node classification, limited labeled data, overfitting mitigation, graph structure exploitation, transductive learning, inductive learning, graph attention networks, graph convolutional networks
